entries.brief: Make the race-base the current branch instead of HEAD~1. The race verifier was comparing against the wrong base, which caused judge timeouts (107s) and incorrect diff output.

Add a diff-driven density gate that blocks a hands-off verdict when the diff increases code size without adding functionality. The gate computes LOC_before - LOC_after and requires density_delta >= 0 for merged_no_intervention.
Build a governance suite with a runnable Definition-of-Done gate. The gate must be executable as a verification step before merge, checking that all DoD criteria are satisfied.
Collapse main.tsx wiring behind a lazy integration seam. Replace eager import and direct function calls with a lazy-load pattern that defers chrome module resolution until the first use.
Consolidate SCHEMA_VERSION_REQUIRED to a single source of truth. Remove duplicate constant declarations across files and ensure all references import from one module.
Ensure the asicode CLI runs under bun, not node. Update the bin shim and all shebang lines in scripts/ to invoke via bun, and verify that no script relies on node-specific globals.
Add a CLI watcher that cleans up worktrees after a PR is merged. When watch-merges detects a merge event, it should run a cleanup step to remove the race-branch worktree.
Fix the L2 self-review invoker so it correctly resolves the production reviewer when the agent is in race mode. The invoker was falling back to a mock reviewer instead of the real one.
Add a --list flag to the status CLI that shows recent briefs. The flag should return structured output with brief id, project, status, and timestamp for each entry.
Wire the L2 reviewer as a production invoker with a dependency factory. The invoker must create reviewer instances from the dependency graph, not from hardcoded paths.
Complete the openclaude to asicode rename across the entire codebase. Update package name, CLI binary name, all CLI flags, environment variable names, file references, and documentation strings.
Add provider scope metadata to plugins so each plugin declares which providers it targets (e.g., 'anthropic', 'ollama', 'all'). The scope should be read from the plugin manifest.
